{"upcomingEventNews":{"headers":["title","content","url","eventDate","originalNewsContent"],"rows":[["Quantum Doomsday Clock predicts Bitcoin encryption break date","The new online Doomsday Clock tool sets March 8, 2028 as the critical date when a cryptographically ","https://finbold.com/new-quantum-doomsday-clock-sets-date-when-bitcoins-encryption-will-be-cracked/","8 March 2028","A new online tool predicts that quantum computers will be able to crack Bitcoin’s private keys by March 8, 2028. As of November 8, 2025, the countdown shows 2 years and 4 months until a cryptographically relevant quantum computer (CRQC) could use Shor’s algorithm to break ECDSA secp256k1, the elliptic curve standard protecting Bitcoin and most cryptocurrencies. The tool was developed by Dr. Richard Carback (cryptography researcher, xx network co-founder) and Colton Dillion (entrepreneur), under Postquant Labs and Hadamard Gate Inc. Their model, based on recent academic research and public quantum hardware roadmaps from IBM, Google, and others, estimates that 1,673 logical qubits would suffice to derive Bitcoin private keys from exposed public keys within a practical timeframe. If achieved, all Bitcoin addresses that have ever spent funds and revealed public keys—including legacy P2PK and reused P2PKH addresses—would become instantly vulnerable. Unspent Taproot (bc1p…) and segregated witness addresses would remain secure longer, as only their hashes are public.

Expert opinions vary: some see the 2028 timeline as aggressive, but institutions like NIST and the Global Risk Institute place the quantum threat window between 2028 and 2035. Google researcher Craig Gidney’s 2025 paper suggests breaking RSA-2048 could require 20 times fewer resources than previously thought, possibly compressing the timeline to 2030–2035. Naoris Protocol CEO David Carvalho warns of risks within five years, by 2030. The Global Risk Institute’s 2024 report, based on 32 expert surveys, estimates a 50% chance of a quantum threat within 15 years (by 2039), with risk rising through the 2030s. Blockstream CEO Adam Back argues there’s no serious threat for at least two decades but acknowledges future breakthroughs could force migration to quantum-resistant addresses. IBM plans to launch the world’s first fault-tolerant quantum computer, Quantum Starling, by 2029, after Google’s 105-qubit Willow chip milestone."],["EU AI Act Becomes Fully Applicable","The EU AI Act, a risk-based legal framework governing AI systems including those used in crypto-trad","https://ambcrypto.com/solanas-breakout-is-tied-to-post-hack-sentiment-and-liquidity-shift-details/","2 August 2026","In 2026, AI crypto trading has diversified into five main product types: fully managed AI systems (e.g., MoneyFlare), no-code rule builders (Coinrule, Cryptohopper), exchange-integrated bots (Pionex), multi-exchange automation terminals (3Commas, Bitsgap, Altrady, Gainium), and open-source/self-hosted frameworks (OctoBot, Kryll). Each serves different user needs, from hands-off automation to customizable infrastructure. The ranking focuses on platforms built specifically for crypto automation, evaluated by crypto-nativeness, automation strength, global usability, clarity, and realism.

Top 10 AI Crypto Trading Bots in 2026:
1. MoneyFlare (UK): Fully managed, beginner-friendly, minimal setup, operated by RICHMOND AI FINANCIAL SERVICES LTD.
2. 3Commas (BVI): Multi-exchange, no-code, DCA/GRID/Signal bots, operated by 3C Trade Tech Ltd.
3. Cryptohopper (Netherlands): Customizable, AI-driven, for intermediate users, operated by Cryptohopper B.V.
4. Coinrule (UK): No-code, rule-based, clarity-focused, operated by Coinrule Limited.
5. Pionex (Lithuania): Exchange-integrated, built-in bots, PionexGPT AI, operated by Pionew UAB.
6. Bitsgap (UAE): Smart orders, analytics, AI assistant, operated by BITSGAP SOFTWARE SOLUTIONS – FZCO.
7. Gainium (US): No-code, open-source, AI layer, operated by Gainium LLC.
8. Altrady (Netherlands): Multi-exchange, automation for advanced traders, operated by Altrady B.V.
9. OctoBot (France): Open-source, self-custody, transparency, operated by Drakkar-Software.
10. Kryll (France): Visual strategy, AI analytics, community-driven, operated by Cryptsense.

Regulation: Most countries regulate crypto trading activity, not AI bots per se. The EU requires compliance with both MiCA (crypto) and the AI Act (fully applicable from 2 August 2026). The US focuses on fraud and securities; the UK’s FCA regime applies from 25 October 2027. Singapore (MAS), Hong Kong (SFC), and UAE (VARA/ADGM) have specific licensing and compliance requirements. The best bot depends on user needs: hands-off (MoneyFlare), multi-exchange (3Commas, Cryptohopper), no-code (Coinrule), exchange-native (Pionex), or open-source (Gainium, OctoBot, Kryll). Choose by model, jurisdiction, and workflow, not just AI marketing. FAQ clarifies bots’ functions, suitability, safety, and coding requirements. Disclaimer: Paid post, not advice."],["GENIUS Act Final Regulation Deadline","Under the GENIUS Act, federal banking agencies must finalize stablecoin regulations by July 18, 2026","https://news.bitcoin.com/fdic-proposes-genius-act-rules-for-bank-stablecoin-issuers-11-reserves-and-2-day-redemptions-required","18 July 2026","The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) approved a notice of proposed rulemaking on April 7, 2026, to implement GENIUS Act standards for bank-affiliated stablecoin issuers, known as permitted payment stablecoin issuers (PPSIs). PPSIs, typically subsidiaries of FDIC-supervised insured depository institutions, must hold 1:1 reserves in eligible assets—such as U.S. currency, bank balances, short-term Treasuries, and certain money market funds—backing all outstanding stablecoins. These reserves must be monitored daily, segregated from other assets, and counterparty exposure is capped at 40% of total reserves. PPSIs must demonstrate the ability to quickly access and convert reserves to cash.

Redemption requests must be fulfilled within two business days, and PPSIs must publicly disclose their redemption policy. For redemptions exceeding 10% of outstanding issuance in 24 hours, PPSIs must notify the FDIC and may request an extension. New PPSIs face a $5 million minimum capital requirement for their first three years, with ongoing capital in common equity tier 1 and additional tier 1 instruments only; no Tier 2 capital is allowed. Parent banks must deconsolidate PPSI subsidiaries for regulatory capital purposes. PPSIs must also maintain a separate pool of highly liquid assets equal to 12 months of operating expenses. Failure to meet capital or liquidity requirements triggers mandatory FDIC notification and possible suspension of new issuance.

The rule requires comprehensive cybersecurity and annual AML/CFT certifications. PPSI reserves held as deposits at insured banks are insured only up to $250,000 as corporate deposits, with no pass-through coverage for individual holders, reflecting the GENIUS Act’s prohibition on deposit insurance for stablecoin holders. Tokenized deposits meeting the legal definition of "deposit" receive standard insurance treatment. The FDIC is accepting public comments for 60 days, with the rule taking effect by Jan. 18, 2027, or 120 days after final regulations, whichever is sooner."],["SEC Decision Deadline for Grayscale ETF Options Proposal","The SEC must approve or disapprove the NYSE American proposal to list options on the Grayscale Coind","https://news.bitcoin.com/sec-opens-proceedings-on-nyse-proposal-to-list-grayscale-crypto-etf-options","11 July 2026","The SEC has initiated proceedings to review NYSE American LLC’s proposal to list options on the Grayscale Coindesk Crypto 5 ETF, which is composed of 76.02% bitcoin and 14.90% ethereum. The proposal, submitted December 29, aims to expand institutional access to digital asset derivatives by allowing trading of physically settled, American-style options tied to this diversified crypto basket. The SEC’s April 9 order, under Release No. 34-105187, launches a structured review process with statutory deadlines: public comments are due 21 days after Federal Register publication, rebuttals within 35 days, and a decision required by July 11, potentially extendable to September 9 for further analysis. NYSE American asserts that existing surveillance and reporting systems can handle the increased trading activity and that infrastructure capacity is sufficient, aligning with current derivatives market standards.

Regulatory scrutiny is focused on whether the proposal meets investor protection and market integrity standards, particularly compliance with Section 6(b)(5) of the Exchange Act, which mandates safeguards against fraudulent and manipulative practices. The SEC’s review mirrors previous hesitations regarding derivatives tied to markets lacking uniform oversight. The Commission has opened a public comment period, inviting industry participants to weigh in on whether the proposed rule change aligns with regulatory requirements. The SEC emphasized, “Institution of proceedings does not indicate that the Commission has reached any conclusions with respect to any of the issues involved.” The outcome of this review could influence future ETF derivatives and is seen as critical for institutional adoption amid growing interest in crypto derivatives. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and SEC Chair Paul Atkins are also urging Congress to advance crypto legislation to secure U.S. market leadership."],["Statutory Effective Date","January 18, 2027 marks the statutory effective date of the GENIUS Act. From this day, any new stable","https://www.kucoin.com/blog/jp-2026-genius-act-the-latest-status-and-update","18 January 2027","The GENIUS Act, signed into law in July 2025, establishes the first federal framework for U.S. payment stablecoins, aiming to integrate them into the regulated banking system. As of March 2026, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) has issued its Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPRM) to operationalize the Act. The Act is set to take full effect by January 18, 2027, or 120 days after final regulations are published, with a three-year grace period ending July 18, 2028, for full compliance.

Key provisions include:
- Stablecoins must be backed 1:1 by high-quality liquid assets (e.g., U.S. Treasuries, central bank reserves).
- Payment stablecoins are reclassified as neither securities nor commodities but as unique instruments under banking regulators (OCC, Federal Reserve).
- Issuers must maintain 100% reserves and provide holders with legal priority in insolvency, similar to bank deposits.
- Strict AML/KYC requirements and technical ability to "freeze and burn" assets upon law enforcement request.
- A "rebuttable presumption" (the "Yield Ban") prohibits issuers and affiliates from paying interest or rewards on stablecoins; indirect rewards are also scrutinized.
- Issuers with over $10 billion in circulation must transition to federal supervision; smaller issuers remain under state-certified regimes.
- Minimum capital floor for issuers is $5 million.

Timeline milestones:
- July 18, 2025: Act enacted.
- February 25, 2026: OCC releases 376-page NPRM detailing capital requirements and licensing.
- May 2026: 60-day comment period for industry feedback ends.
- January 18, 2027: Statutory effective date; new issuances must comply.
- July 18, 2028: All secondary market trading must be in compliant tokens.

The Act brings legitimacy and user protection but centralizes the market, raises compliance costs, and restricts yield opportunities. U.S. exchanges must delist non-compliant stablecoins by 2027. The Act applies only to payment stablecoins, not all cryptocurrencies."],["GENIUS Act comes into effect","The GENIUS Act, signed into law in July 2025 to regulate stablecoin issuers, is scheduled to take e","https://coinmarketcap.com/community/articles/69f4d635bb34324263e1af36","18 January 2027","Major US banking trade groups, including the American Bankers Association and the Bank Policy Institute, have formally requested that the Treasury Department and FDIC pause three GENIUS Act rulemaking comment periods until 60 days after the OCC finalizes its primary stablecoin framework, citing that the Treasury’s equivalency rule, the FDIC’s issuer standards rule, and the FinCEN-OFAC anti-money-laundering directive are all “substantively tethered” to the OCC’s pending framework and cannot be meaningfully assessed until its publication. This pushback was made official on April 22, with the groups arguing the proposals are structurally interdependent. The GENIUS Act, signed into law in July 2025, is set to take effect by January 18, 2027. 

Simultaneously, stablecoin startup Agora filed for a national trust bank charter with the OCC on April 24, aiming to establish a federal presence before the rules are finalized. Agora CEO Nick van Eck described the banks’ stance as unsurprising, attributing their concerns to potential deposit flight and the erosion of the yield spread between near-zero deposit rates and returns on Fed reserves. Van Eck stated that if Agora’s charter is approved by year-end, the company could issue stablecoins directly under federal oversight, eliminating “egregious fees” in fiat-to-crypto on/off ramps and expanding into custody, compliance, and payments.

The OCC released its proposed stablecoin rulebook in February 2026, covering issuance, reserves, supervision, and redemption requirements, with a 60-day comment window closing May 1. The Treasury separately proposed rules for state-level oversight of issuers under $10 billion, with a June 2 comment deadline. Banks are seeking to align the three regulatory timelines, which could delay the GENIUS Act’s activation by several months and give traditional lenders more time to assess competition from nonbank stablecoin issuers."],["Final FDIC Stablecoin Regulations Take Effect","The FDIC’s proposed prudential framework under the GENIUS Act is expected to become enforceable on  ","https://www.gate.com/blog/101706/genius-act-implementation-fdic-proposed-rules-reshape-us-payment-stablecoin-regulation","18 January 2027","On April 7, 2026, the FDIC Board approved a Notice of Proposed Rulemaking to establish a prudential framework for FDIC-supervised payment stablecoin issuers under the GENIUS Act, signed into law July 2025. The 191-page proposal, open for 60 days of public comment (until June 9, 2026), sets final regulations to take effect January 18, 2027. It covers five key areas: reserve assets, redemption mechanisms, capital requirements, risk management, and custody standards, and poses 144 questions for industry feedback. Issuers must fully back stablecoins 1:1 with qualified assets (US currency, FDIC-insured deposits, short-term US Treasuries), segregate reserves from operational funds, and monitor daily. Redemption requests must be processed within two business days; withdrawals exceeding 10% of outstanding coins require regulator notification. PPSI subsidiaries must maintain $5 million minimum capital for three years and a liquidity buffer covering 12 months of expenses, separate from reserves. AML and cybersecurity certifications, independent audits, and reviews are required. Stablecoin holders are explicitly excluded from FDIC deposit insurance; issuers cannot claim tokens pay interest or returns, nor imply insurance coverage. Tokenized deposits meeting legal definitions are insured, but their regulatory path differs. The GENIUS Act enables a dual federal-state structure: issuers under $10 billion can opt for certified state regulation; above $10 billion, federal supervision is mandatory within 18 months. The FDIC proposal operates federally, while OCC and Treasury rules complement with broader issuer coverage and state certification criteria. Industry disputes persist over return payments to holders; GENIUS Act prohibits issuer-paid returns, but platform rewards remain possible, sparking debate and White House involvement. As of April 13, 2026, global stablecoin market cap reached $318.6 billion (up 150% since 2024), with USDT at $184.4 billion, USDC at $78.6 billion, and monthly on-chain transfers at $10.3 trillion. The FDIC’s rulemaking marks a shift from legislative authorization to regulatory enforcement, shaping the future of US payment stablecoin oversight."],["Fed Interest Rate Decision Announcement","The Fed’s interest rate decision announcement on June 17 will be the first under a new central bank","https://finance.yahoo.com/news/trader-bets-swell-trump-backed-102329374.html","17 June 2026","Traders are increasing bets that a new Federal Reserve chair—likely to be Kevin Hassett, as indicated by Donald Trump—plus delayed economic data this month, will support Trump’s push for lower US interest rates. Futures market demand is rising for short-term curve structures tied to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR), reflecting expectations of accelerated monetary policy easing after Jerome Powell’s term ends in May. The first Fed policy announcement under the new chair is set for June 17. Trump stated the Fed chair selection is “down to one,” naming Hassett as a “potential Fed chair,” with a decision expected early next year. Kristina Hooper of Man Group warned this could create a “shadow Fed chair,” complicating Fed communications and market clarity.

A major SOFR futures fly trade—the largest in over a year—was executed Monday, with heightened activity in 3-, 6-, and 12-month SOFR spreads as traders anticipate more rate cuts. Goldman Sachs strategists are hedging ahead of November labor data (due Dec. 16, delayed by the government shutdown), which could reinforce dovish bets if it confirms labor market softening. They recommend positions favoring curve steepening, such as SOFR Dec26/Dec27 spreads and 2s10s bull steepeners, especially if Hassett is confirmed.

Dovish policy expectations and increased December rate-cut odds pushed 10-year Treasury yields below 4% last week; on Wednesday, yields were at 4.08%, down from 4.11% Tuesday. Jack McIntyre of Brandywine Global noted that rate cuts with inflation above target could raise long-end yields even as short-term rates fall, predicting “bear steepening” if Hassett is confirmed.

For the week ending Dec. 1, investor net long positions dropped to their lowest since Nov. 3. SOFR options activity is concentrated around the 96.25 and 96.3125 strikes, with traders positioning for the Dec. 10 policy meeting, where 22 basis points of rate cuts are priced in. Option premiums remain near neutral, with a slight bias toward calls."],["EU sanctions package #21 to be finalized","The European Commission will finalize its 21st sanctions package on July 15, 2026, targeting 20 non-","https://coingeek.com/will-iran-follow-russias-stablecoin-sanctions-playbook/","15 July 2026","The European Commission (EC) will release its 21st sanctions package against Russia by July 15, targeting 20 banks, crypto firms, platforms, and oil traders in third countries aiding sanctioned Russian entities. For the first time, a full third-country ban for crypto-asset services is proposed. The UK recently sanctioned HTX (formerly Huobi) and the A7 Network, which operates the ruble-backed stablecoin A7A5, central to Russia’s sanctions-evasion. Despite UK/EU/US sanctions, A7A5 has grown rapidly since its January 2025 launch: over $110 billion in cumulative on-chain transactions, ~43% of the global non-USD stablecoin market, and 29,000 holders by May 2026. CertiK attributes A7A5’s resilience to its non-Western user base and off-chain instruments like ‘Digital Promissory Notes’ that facilitate cash exchanges via Telegram bots, complicating tracking. A7 Network is partially owned by Russia’s Promsvyazbank, which passes 50% of ruble interest to token holders, incentivizing holding over transacting.

After Tether froze $28 million USDT linked to Garantex in March 2025, Russia shifted to A7A5, mostly on TRON, with peak daily transfers of $1.7 billion. The Kremlin retaliated against UK media figures, including Alexander Browder, whose report on A7A5’s role in Russian money laundering spurred UK sanctions. Browder called his sanction "a badge of honor," claiming A7A5 is Russia’s "Achilles’ heel."

Iran, facing similar US-led freezes ($344 million USDT in April; claims of $1 billion seized in May), may emulate Russia’s stablecoin strategy. OFAC recently sanctioned four Iranian exchanges and warned against paying Iran’s Hormuz Strait tolls in USDT or yuan. Iran’s spokesperson responded with Shakespearean ambiguity. The article suggests Iran could develop a rial-based stablecoin to bypass Western enforcement, following Russia’s model."],["Presidential Signing of the CLARITY Act","The White House is targeting July 4, 2026, for President Trump to sign the CLARITY Act into law, cod","https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/happens-crypto-clarity-act-becomes-093057196.html","4 July 2026","The CLARITY Act, targeting a July 4, 2026 signing, would permanently classify every crypto asset as a digital commodity, investment contract asset, or payment stablecoin, ending the SEC’s ability to sue exchanges and token issuers without warning and resolving the decade-long SEC-CFTC jurisdictional fight. The Act cleared the Senate Banking Committee on May 14, but still requires 60 Senate votes, House reconciliation, and the President’s signature; Polymarket estimates a 59% chance of passage in 2026. JPMorgan calls the bill a “positive catalyst” for digital assets, predicting a market surge in H2 2026. Senator Cynthia Lummis warns that failure to pass before the November midterms means no new chance until at least 2030, leaving the industry in regulatory uncertainty.

The Act codifies CFTC jurisdiction over digital commodities like Bitcoin and Ethereum, SEC oversight of investment contract assets, and banking regulation for payment stablecoins. It gives statutory certainty to assets like Solana, Avalanche, and Cardano, enabling the SEC to approve their spot ETFs and unlocking institutional capital. XRP gains permanent commodity status, with Standard Chartered projecting $4-$8 billion in new XRP ETF inflows and Ripple’s On-Demand Liquidity business able to expand as 60% of RippleNet’s 300 banking partners could shift to XRP settlement. Non-custodial DeFi developers receive a safe harbor from SEC enforcement, encouraging U.S.-based innovation. The Act also provides a legal framework for tokenized real-world assets, allowing institutions like the DTCC to move $2 quadrillion in annual clearing volume on-chain.

If the bill fails or is delayed, crypto prices would fall in stages as the market prices out the 60-65% passage probability, with Bitcoin risking a drop below $70,000 and XRP retracing toward $1.10-$1.20. The SEC retains broad enforcement powers, and institutional adoption stalls, which Lummis calls a “regulatory dark ages.”"],["Bitcoin reaches $200,000 by December 31, 2026","A prediction market currently sits at YES for Bitcoin hitting $200,000 by December 31, 2026, with a ","https://cryptobriefing.com/czech-national-bank-eyes-1-bitcoin-reserve-allocation-for-improved-returns/","31 December 2026","Czech National Bank Governor Aleš Michl has endorsed allocating 1% of central bank reserves to Bitcoin, arguing this would improve returns without increasing risk. This follows his earlier January 2025 proposal for up to a 5% allocation, but the 1% figure is seen as more acceptable to the cautious Bank Board. The Czech National Bank already operates a $1 million experimental digital asset portfolio, separate from its core reserves. If implemented, the Czech Republic would become the first European central bank to hold Bitcoin, potentially pressuring other EU nations to follow, despite opposition from ECB President Christine Lagarde. The main rationale is Bitcoin’s low correlation with traditional assets like bonds, supporting diversification. On the prediction market Polymarket, the contract for "Bitcoin reaching $200,000 by December 31, 2026" is currently at YES, with little movement over the past week except for a 45-point drop at 12:16 PM. The market is thin, as $5,264 is required to move the odds by 5 points, and daily trading volume is just $280 in USDC. Traders appear to be waiting for concrete central bank actions rather than statements. A YES share pays $1 if Bitcoin hits $200,000 by the end of 2026, but this outcome likely depends on more central banks adopting Bitcoin or significant regulatory changes. Future market movement could be triggered by announcements from other European central banks, shifts in ECB policy, or changes in Bitcoin’s correlation with traditional assets. The article also references related crypto and market prediction topics but focuses on the Czech National Bank’s potential Bitcoin reserve allocation and its implications for the broader market."],["FOMC Meeting","From June 16 to 17, 2026, the Federal Open Market Committee will convene to decide on interest rates","https://msx.com/news-details/weeklyreport608","16 June 2026","Last week, the U.S. stock market shifted from chasing AI-driven growth to reassessing the cost of capital amid strong labor data, rising oil prices, and stretched AI valuations. The Nasdaq fell 4.49% for the week, with semiconductors plunging Friday, while energy, healthcare, and consumer staples were resilient. The S&P 500 dropped 2.72%, Russell 2000 lost 3.38%, and the Dow declined 0.30%. The 2-year Treasury yield rose to ~4.15%, the 10-year to ~4.54%, and the U.S. Dollar Index strengthened. Crude oil posted weekly gains, but gold and silver weakened; Bitcoin and ETH also fell, reflecting risk-asset behavior under higher rates and a strong dollar.

The May nonfarm payrolls report showed 172,000 jobs added, 4.3% unemployment, and wage growth of 0.3% MoM/3.4% YoY, making Fed rate cuts less likely. Rising oil prices, driven by Middle East tensions and Strait of Hormuz risks, fueled inflation concerns, further limiting Fed policy flexibility. High-valuation tech stocks, especially semiconductors (Broadcom, Nvidia, AMD, Micron, Qualcomm, Marvell), faced valuation pressure as investors demanded not just strong growth but continued outperformance. Consumer discretionary (Lululemon) and communication services also weakened due to high rates and AI CapEx. Precious metals and crypto remained under pressure from the strong dollar and high rates.

This week’s focus is on May CPI (Wed 8:30 ET), PPI (Thu 8:30 ET), Oracle, Adobe, and Apple earnings, and the SpaceX IPO. Key variables: 2-year yield near 4.15%, WTI oil above $90, Nasdaq/semiconductor stability, and SpaceX IPO reception. Persistent inflation, strong oil, and high rates would pressure tech and crypto; cooling inflation and strong tech earnings could spark recovery. MSX will monitor these themes and asset performance."],["MiCA Fully Enforced","On July 1, 2026, the EU’s Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation comes into full force, imposing","https://www.mexc.com/en-NG/news/384907","1 July 2026","The crypto industry enters 2026 amid rapid evolution and a shifting global macro environment, with new regulatory frameworks being gradually implemented and structural changes underway. Mainstream adoption is advancing, project upgrades and token generation events (TGEs) are expected, and ongoing regulatory policy developments in multiple countries may significantly impact market sentiment. PANews presents a 2026 Web3 events calendar to help users track key dates for portfolio and strategy planning.

Special focus events throughout 2026 include: January 3, the 17th anniversary of the Bitcoin mainnet launch; Federal Reserve FOMC interest rate meetings in January, March, June, September, and December; a Q1 vote on the Clarity Act; the first batch of Hong Kong stablecoin licenses issued in Q1; Token2049 and Hong Kong Carnival in April and October; the World Cup starting June 11; MiCA (Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation) full enforcement on July 1; Ethereum Glamsterdam and Hegota hard forks; U.S. midterm elections on November 3; and the announcement of a new Federal Reserve Chairman.

Key January 2026 events: January 1, the digital yuan wallet launches an interest accrual function; January 3, Bitcoin mainnet's 17th anniversary; January 8, Ethereum core developers meet to discuss the Hegota upgrade; January 9, U.S. unemployment and non-farm payroll data released; January 15, MSCI releases the Digital Asset General Classification (DAT) for the first time; January 20, Zama TGE; January 27-28, Federal Reserve FOMC meeting; January 29, Powell holds a monetary policy press conference.

PANews offers a downloadable encrypted calendar with comprehensive coverage and flexible filtering for these events. The article is informational and does not constitute financial advice."],["TikChain Token Listing and Launch","On July 1, 2026, internal TIK transfers, exchange listings, and the full TikWallet launch activate, ","https://coinmarketcap.com/community/articles/6a2811dc11a9ab1bd7aa1814","1 July 2026","TikChain Network will launch its TIK token on July 1, 2026, making tokens tradable for 300,000+ miners. The team has met all prior milestones on schedule. As of June 9, 2026, KYC Phase 1 closed due to server overload from high registrations; Phase 2 will be integrated directly into the TIK mining app for a faster, simpler process. Miners who completed KYC in Phase 1 (around June 2) do not need to repeat verification. The KYC deadline is June 30, 2026; missing it means no July 1 access. NFT campaigns are now live in the app, boosting engagement before listing. The team confirmed a portion of user balances may be locked at launch to protect price stability, with details to be announced before July 1. If 20–30% of community supply is locked, day-one sell pressure will drop; if not, up to 2.67 billion tokens could enter the market immediately, risking price volatility. No exchange partners have been officially named as of June 9, but internal transfers and exchange listings will activate simultaneously on July 1. TikWallet, the ecosystem’s social wallet, launches in July on Google Play and App Store. Tokenomics: total supply is either 100 million or 3,333,333,333 (most recent figure), with 80% for miners, 10% for the team, and 10% for liquidity/ecosystem. Price projections for day one range from $0.01–$1.00, depending on balance locks and exchange depth. The roadmap includes node deployment, NFT tools, token creation, AI integrations, DEX preview, and creator programs post-July. Risks: high float at launch, balance lock uncertainty, KYC timing, unconfirmed exchanges, and no price history. Opportunities: large user base, social growth (50,000 X followers), NFT engagement, zero gas fees, and a strong delivery record. This is not financial advice; TIK is a high-risk pre-listing token."],["Succinct (PROVE) Token Unlock","Succinct will unlock 208,330,000 PROVE tokens on August 5, 2026, constituting approximately 104.17% ","https://www.gate.com/learn/articles/wlfi-world-liberty-financial-explained-beginners-guide-to-the-token-and-listing-progress/11416","5 August 2026","WLFI (World Liberty Financial) is a governance-focused token aiming to establish a decentralized financial governance framework, empowering holders to participate in decision-making and voting. Unlike BTC or ETH, WLFI prioritized governance utility over market trading, initially issuing the token as non-transferable. In July 2025, a community vote passed with 99.94% approval to unlock the token for trading, but as of March 29, 2026, WLFI remains unlisted on any major exchange and cannot be freely bought or sold.

WLFI plans to list on Nasdaq with a $1.5 billion market capitalization via a reverse merger structure using ALT5 Sigma enterprise treasury. ALT5 Sigma will hold about 7.5% of WLFI’s total supply, allowing traditional investors to gain indirect exposure to WLFI by purchasing ALT5-related stocks, bypassing the need for a crypto wallet. The project team has not announced a specific listing date, nor clarified whether WLFI will first appear on decentralized (DEX) or centralized exchanges (CEX), or how liquidity will be provided.

Key risks include: (1) Not Yet Listed—WLFI currently has a price of 0 and all investment prospects are speculative; (2) Regulatory Uncertainty—the SEC has not classified WLFI as a security, and policies could change; (3) Speculative Hype—media attention, partly due to connections with the Trump family, may drive sentiment-fueled volatility. 

Advice for beginners: learn about the project before participating, wait for an actual listing and observe price/liquidity trends, and never invest more than you can afford to lose. In summary, WLFI is a unique token with political and financial innovation potential, but remains in a pre-listing phase. Now is a time for research and observation rather than speculative investment. (Author: Max)"]]}}
